message:
feat(ftui-backend,ftui-tty): add backend trait crate + native TTY skeleton (bd-lff4p.4.1)

Introduce two new crates per ADR-008 (terminal backend strategy):

- ftui-backend: Backend, BackendClock, BackendEventSource, BackendPresenter
  traits and BackendFeatures config struct. This defines the platform
  abstraction boundary between ftui-runtime and I/O implementations.

- ftui-tty: Skeleton native Unix backend implementing the ftui-backend traits
  (TtyBackend, TtyClock, TtyEventSource, TtyPresenter). #![forbid(unsafe_code)].
  Concrete I/O deferred to bd-lff4p.4.2/4.3/4.4.

8 unit tests. Unblocks bd-lff4p.4.2, 4.3, 4.4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add fuzz targets to harden the parser and mutation logic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzz harnesses run locally and in CI (time-bounded) with crash artifacts captured.
- Any found crashes become regression fixtures in the conformance corpus.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-core (API skeleton + workspace wiring)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-core` with the minimal public API surface and internal module layout.

Notes
- Keep dependencies minimal.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core` passes (native).
- Basic types compile: Grid, Cell, Parser, Patch.
","notes":"Implemented crate skeleton in crates/frankenterm-core (Cell/SgrAttrs, Cursor+SavedCursor, Grid ops, Modes flags, Parser skeleton emitting Actions, Patch + Scrollback skeleton). Wired into workspace Cargo.toml. Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core, cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512397161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512374489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).

- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.

- Implement selection primitives (char/cell/word/line) and copy extraction from grid+scrollback.

- Word selection heuristics tuned for code and paths.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","title":"Implement: VT/ANSI parser state machine (CSI/OSC/SGR + edits)","description":"Goal
- Implement the output stream parser and apply mutations to the grid.

Must Support (initial)
- CSI cursor moves, erase in line/screen, insert/delete char/line, scroll up/down, set scroll region.
- SGR attributes and colors (16/256/truecolor).
- OSC 0/2 (title) (optional storage) and OSC 8 hyperlinks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance fixtures from bd-lff4p.1.1 replay to exact expected grid snapshots.
- Proptest: parser never panics on arbitrary byte streams.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","title":"Implement: incremental patch API for renderer (dirty spans, diff runs)","description":"Goal

Requirements

- Stable, deterministic patch ordering.
- APIs designed for both WebGPU and native present paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks demonstrate sub-linear updates for typical workloads.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","title":"Test: proptest invariants for grid + parser (never panic; stable semantics)","description":"Goal
- Add property tests that make it safe to aggressively optimize and refactor.

Invariants (examples)
- Cursor always within bounds.
- Lines never exceed width in cell count.
- Parser accepts arbitrary bytes without panic.
- Applying a patch preserves semantic equality vs full recompute.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run in CI with fixed seeds + shrinkable counterexamples.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10","title":"FrankenTerm Remote: bidirectional protocol + PTY/websocket bridge","description":"Why
- Replacing xterm.js means we must support the full bidirectional terminal contract:
  - output parsing (VT/ANSI)
  - input encoding (keyboard/mouse -> bytes)
  - terminal replies to application queries (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - transport to a real PTY (local or remote)

Scope
- A minimal but correct PTY bridge (Rust) that can run locally and in production contexts.
- A secure websocket protocol for browser <-> PTY.
- End-to-end demos: connect FrankenTerm.WASM to a real shell and run scripted sessions.

Non-Negotiables
- Deterministic trace logging (JSONL) for every E2E session.
- Security: origin restrictions, explicit allowlists, no arbitrary command execution in prod.

Success Criteria
- Browser terminal can connect to a PTY bridge, run commands, and render accurately.
- Golden transcript tests prevent regressions.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm websocket protocol + threat model","description":"Goal
- Define the browser <-> PTY transport protocol and a production-safe threat model.

Must Cover
- Message types
  - input bytes / semantic events
  - resize
  - output chunks
  - terminal replies (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - clipboard (optional)
  - keepalive
- Versioning + schema
  - protocol version negotiation
  - JSON Schema / binary schema (explicit)
- Backpressure + flow control (prevent OOM)
  - bounded queues
  - coalescing policies (resize storms, mouse move floods)
  - fairness: interactive input must not be starved by output
- Security model
  - origin restrictions, auth, rate limits
  - allowed commands (if any), sandboxing
  - no implicit file access
  - explicit threat model + mitigations
- Capability identity
  - TERM/capability profile story for remote PTY sessions (ties to terminfo bead)
- Logging
  - every session emits JSONL with run_id/session_id, git_sha/build_id, sizes, wire counters, latency histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is versioned and has a schema.
- E2E scripts can generate deterministic transcripts for golden checks.
- Threat model includes an explicit loss matrix (false allow vs false block) and a documented decision policy.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:57.868100267Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1370,7 +1370,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.8","title":"Test: wasm-bindgen-test coverage for ftui-web runner","description":"Goal
- Add WASM-side tests for core event and rendering flows.

Acceptance Criteria

- A small set of end-to-end render checksums run under wasm test runner.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","title":"Optimize: wasm binary size + startup time (LTO, feature gating)","description":"Goal
- Make the web demo load fast and keep wasm artifacts small.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured size targets with before/after numbers.
- Startup (time-to-first-frame) improved with profiling evidence.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4","title":"ftui-native backend: replace Crossterm (Unix-first)","description":"Scope
- Replace Crossterm with a first-party backend: raw mode, alt-screen, mouse, bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard, resize, and robust cleanup.
- Drive input via ftui-core InputParser on raw bytes (no Crossterm event translation).

Deliverables
- New backend crate (name TBD) and integration into ftui-core/ftui-runtime.
- PTY + panic-cleanup tests; perf improvements via fewer syscalls + tighter buffering.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Unix-first (Linux/macOS). Windows later.

Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal API compiles and can be used by ftui-core/ftui-runtime via a backend trait.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in the crate.

- Unix-first (Linux/macOS). Windows later.

- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in the crate.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","title":"Implement: Unix raw mode + terminal feature toggles (alt-screen, mouse, paste, focus, kitty)","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm terminal mode management with our own, with stronger semantics and fewer surprises.

Requirements
- Raw mode enter/exit.
- Alt screen enter/leave.
- Mouse capture enable/disable.
- Bracketed paste + focus events.
- Kitty keyboard protocol enable/disable.
- Robust Drop-based cleanup + panic safety.

Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests prove cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- No leaked terminal state (cursor visible, raw mode off, alt-screen restored).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","title":"Implement: Unix input reader (poll + bytes) feeding ftui-core InputParser","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm event polling with a tight byte-reader + ftui-core parsing.

Requirements
- Non-blocking or poll-based read with timeout.
- Convert bytes to ftui-core Event via existing InputParser.
- Support resize, focus, bracketed paste, mouse sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Input parity tests vs current behavior for core keys and mouse sequences.
- No busy-looping when idle.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","title":"Implement: resize detection + size queries (SIGWINCH)","description":"Goal
- Provide accurate, low-latency resize handling without Crossterm.

Requirements
- Query terminal size via ioctl.
- Deliver resize events via SIGWINCH or equivalent.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test: resize storm does not drop or reorder events; no panics.

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+[package]
+name = "ftui-backend"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+license = "MIT"
+description = "Backend traits for FrankenTUI: platform abstraction for input, presentation, and time."
+repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui"
+homepage = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui"
+documentation = "https://docs.rs/ftui-backend"
+readme = "../../README.md"
+
+[dependencies]
+ftui-core = { path = "../ftui-core", version = "0.1.1" }
+ftui-render = { path = "../ftui-render", version = "0.1.1" }

@@ -0,0 +1,126 @@
+#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
+#![doc = "Backend traits for FrankenTUI: platform abstraction for input, presentation, and time."]
+#![doc = ""]
+#![doc = "This crate defines the boundary between the ftui runtime and platform-specific"]
+#![doc = "implementations (native terminal via `ftui-tty`, WASM via `ftui-web`)."]
+#![doc = ""]
+#![doc = "See ADR-008 for the design rationale."]
+
+use core::time::Duration;
+
+use ftui_core::event::Event;
+use ftui_core::terminal_capabilities::TerminalCapabilities;
+use ftui_render::buffer::Buffer;
+use ftui_render::diff::BufferDiff;
+
+/// Terminal feature toggles that backends must support.
+///
+/// These map to terminal modes that are enabled/disabled at session start/end.
+/// Backends translate these into platform-specific escape sequences or API calls.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub struct BackendFeatures {
+    /// SGR mouse capture (CSI ? 1000;1002;1006 h/l on native).
+    pub mouse_capture: bool,
+    /// Bracketed paste mode (CSI ? 2004 h/l on native).
+    pub bracketed_paste: bool,
+    /// Focus-in/focus-out reporting (CSI ? 1004 h/l on native).
+    pub focus_events: bool,
+    /// Kitty keyboard protocol (CSI > 15 u on native).
+    pub kitty_keyboard: bool,
+}
+
+/// Monotonic clock abstraction.
+///
+/// Native backends use `std::time::Instant`; WASM backends use `performance.now()`.
+/// The runtime never calls `Instant::now()` directly — all time flows through this trait.
+pub trait BackendClock {
+    /// Returns elapsed time since an unspecified epoch, monotonically increasing.
+    fn now_mono(&self) -> Duration;
+}
+
+/// Event source abstraction: terminal size queries, feature toggles, and event I/O.
+///
+/// This is the input half of the backend boundary. The runtime polls this for
+/// canonical `Event` values without knowing whether they come from crossterm,
+/// raw Unix reads, or DOM events.
+pub trait BackendEventSource {
+    /// Platform-specific error type.
+    type Error: core::fmt::Debug + core::fmt::Display;
+
+    /// Query current terminal dimensions (columns, rows).
+    fn size(&self) -> Result<(u16, u16), Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Enable or disable terminal features (mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard).
+    ///
+    /// Backends must track current state and only emit escape sequences for changes.
+    fn set_features(&mut self, features: BackendFeatures) -> Result<(), Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Poll for an available event, returning `true` if one is ready.
+    ///
+    /// Must not block longer than `timeout`. Returns `Ok(false)` on timeout.
+    fn poll_event(&mut self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<bool, Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Read the next available event, or `None` if none is ready.
+    ///
+    /// Call after `poll_event` returns `true`, or speculatively.
+    fn read_event(&mut self) -> Result<Option<Event>, Self::Error>;
+}
+
+/// Presentation abstraction: UI rendering and log output.
+///
+/// This is the output half of the backend boundary. The runtime hands a `Buffer`
+/// (and optional `BufferDiff`) to the presenter, which emits platform-specific
+/// output (ANSI escape sequences on native, DOM mutations on web).
+pub trait BackendPresenter {
+    /// Platform-specific error type.
+    type Error: core::fmt::Debug + core::fmt::Display;
+
+    /// Terminal capabilities detected by this backend.
+    fn capabilities(&self) -> &TerminalCapabilities;
+
+    /// Write a log line to the scrollback region (inline mode) or stderr.
+    fn write_log(&mut self, text: &str) -> Result<(), Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Present a UI frame.
+    ///
+    /// - `buf`: the full rendered buffer for this frame.
+    /// - `diff`: optional pre-computed diff (backends may recompute if `None`).
+    /// - `full_repaint_hint`: if `true`, the backend should skip diffing and repaint everything.
+    fn present_ui(
+        &mut self,
+        buf: &Buffer,
+        diff: Option<&BufferDiff>,
+        full_repaint_hint: bool,
+    ) -> Result<(), Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Optional: release resources held by the presenter (e.g., grapheme pool compaction).
+    fn gc(&mut self) {}
+}
+
+/// Unified backend combining clock, event source, and presenter.
+///
+/// The `Program` runtime is generic over this trait. Concrete implementations:
+/// - `ftui-tty`: native Unix/macOS terminal (and eventually Windows).
+/// - `ftui-web`: WASM + DOM + WebGPU renderer.
+pub trait Backend {
+    /// Platform-specific error type shared across sub-traits.
+    type Error: core::fmt::Debug + core::fmt::Display;
+
+    /// Clock implementation.
+    type Clock: BackendClock;
+
+    /// Event source implementation.
+    type Events: BackendEventSource<Error = Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Presenter implementation.
+    type Presenter: BackendPresenter<Error = Self::Error>;
+
+    /// Access the monotonic clock.
+    fn clock(&self) -> &Self::Clock;
+
+    /// Access the event source (mutable for polling/reading).
+    fn events(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Events;
+
+    /// Access the presenter (mutable for rendering).
+    fn presenter(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Presenter;
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+[package]
+name = "ftui-tty"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+license = "MIT"
+description = "Native Unix terminal backend for FrankenTUI (replaces Crossterm)."
+repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui"
+homepage = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui"
+documentation = "https://docs.rs/ftui-tty"
+readme = "../../README.md"
+
+[dependencies]
+ftui-backend = { path = "../ftui-backend", version = "0.1.0" }
+ftui-core = { path = "../ftui-core", version = "0.1.1" }
+ftui-render = { path = "../ftui-render", version = "0.1.1" }
